chunk_summary: Guidelines for using corrected age and transitioning growth charts for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants post-discharge, emphasizing consistency, proper measurement techniques, and age-appropriate growth standards.
learning_objectives: Understand how to calculate and apply corrected age for preterm infants.
Learn the appropriate use and transition of growth charts for preterm and ELBW infants.
Recognize the importance of consistent growth monitoring and documentation.
Identify key measurement techniques to ensure accurate growth assessment.
key_facts: Corrected age = chronological age - (40 weeks - gestational age at birth).
Use corrected age for growth and developmental interpretation up to 24 months, or 36 months for ELBW/extremely preterm infants if needed.
Transition from preterm growth charts (e.g., Fenton) to WHO 0–24 month standards around term-equivalent age.
Intergrowth-21st standards can be used up to 64 weeks postmenstrual age (PMA).
Consistency in growth chart use is critical to avoid misinterpreting centile drops as growth failure.
Verify measurement techniques (e.g., naked weight, infantometer for length, correct head circumference landmarks) at every visit.
reference_quote: Corrected age = chronological age - (40 weeks - GA at birth). Plot weight, length, and head circumference using corrected age, not chronological age, and document corrected age in every follow-up note.
document_key_topics: Growth chart transitions for preterm infants
